DocumentationExampleSection.Body: Generate dependency-free consumer POCOs for read-only application use, or include the tooling surface when a CLI, service, or generator needs to load a workspace and work with a typed model object.
The tooling view loads the workspace XML into a fully populated typed model. Use it for generators and command-line tools that need to inspect or mutate workspaces through generated C# classes.
DocumentationExampleSection.Title: Optional tooling surface

DocumentationExampleSection.Body: Use the tooling form when another CLI, service, or generator needs to load a workspace and work with strongly typed model objects.
Generate SQL consumables when the next stage wants relational DDL, foreign keys, and repeatable data scripts rather than XML workspace files.
Use instance diff when two branches or workspaces evolved independently and you want a deterministic artifact describing the right-hand workspace changes.
After landing CSV files as scalar Id fields, inspect deterministic relationship suggestions and promote exact Id-to-Id references into modeled relationships.
